invariants.invariant: TDD: Write failing test FIRST, then implement. For pure regression guards (factor parity tests where configs are already correct), RED means the test file does not yet exist. Document explicitly when RED→GREEN is immediate.

BANNED: import re, regex patterns for structured data. YAML → yaml.safe_load(). Python source → ast.parse()/ast.walk(). JSON → json.loads(). String splitting → str.split(), str.splitlines(), str.partition(). YAML setup block → str.splitlines() then per-line str.split().
Loud failures. NEVER return {}, [], None silently. Always raise ValueError/RuntimeError or logger.error() on invalid input.
ZERO hardcoded numeric parameters. Thresholds from yaml_contract.yaml or config. Tests assert against config defaults, never magic numbers.

STOP POINT: Never modify declarative configs (YAML, TOML) without explicit user authorization. If a task requires a config change, verify the issue number authorizes it, then note "Authorized by #NNN."
ZERO xfails. If a test cannot be implemented without user authorization, use pytest.skip("Blocked: reason") not @pytest.mark.xfail. An xfail that will never pass is not TDD — it is a disabled test.
from __future__ import annotations at top of every Python file.

pre-execution.note: Do not begin Phase 1 unless baseline is clean.

phase.name: P0 Guard Tests
phase.description: Four P0 tests that catch failures before launch. All must pass before Phase 2 begins. Tasks 1.1 and 1.3 run first (independent files). Tasks 1.2 and 1.4 MUST be sequential: both touch preflight_gcp.py. Order: 1.1 parallel with 1.3 → then 1.2 → then 1.4.

task.title: Debug=Production factor consistency guard
task.description:
Guard test ensuring debug.yaml and paper_full.yaml have IDENTICAL factors, model_overrides, and zero_shot_baselines. Only fixed.* parameters may differ. This is a REGRESSION GUARD — configs are currently in sync, so RED means "test file doesn't exist yet." The test goes GREEN immediately because configs are already correct. Document this explicitly in the test module docstring.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: model_overrides MUST be in the comparison (not just training/post_training/analysis factors). A mismatch in model_overrides means debug runs with different batch sizes than production.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Use sorted() equality for list comparison, not set() — ordering matters for deterministic condition-name generation.

tdd-approach.red: Test file does not exist → collection error. Run: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/config/test_debug_production_factor_consistency.py Expected: "no tests ran" or import error.
tdd-approach.green:
Create test file with 6 test classes:
COMPARED_PATHS = [ "factors.training.model_family", "factors.training.loss_name", "factors.training.aux_calibration", "factors.post_training.method", "factors.post_training.recalibration", "factors.analysis.ensemble_strategy", "model_overrides", # REQUIRED: batch_size consistency "zero_shot_baselines", # REQUIRED: baseline consistency ]
ALLOWED_TO_DIFFER = [ "fixed.max_epochs", "fixed.num_folds", "fixed.max_train_volumes", "fixed.max_val_volumes", "debug", # Only debug.yaml has debug: true "experiment_name", # Different names are expected "description", "splits_file", # debug uses debug_half_1fold.json ]
Tests: - test_factors_identical_[param] — parametrized over COMPARED_PATHS Use sorted() for list comparison (order matters for condition names) - test_model_overrides_identical — compare full model_overrides dict - test_zero_shot_baselines_identical — compare full list - test_debug_flag_only_in_debug — debug.yaml has debug: true, paper_full.yaml has no debug key or debug: false - test_paper_has_no_data_limits — paper_full.yaml has no max_train_volumes / max_val_volumes in fixed section
tdd-approach.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/config/test_debug_production_factor_consistency.py -v Expected: all PASS, 0 xfail, 0 skip

task.title: GPU quota preflight check (touches preflight_gcp.py — runs AFTER 1.4)
task.description:
Add check_gcp_gpu_quota() to scripts/preflight_gcp.py. Must run after task 1.4 to avoid merge conflicts on the same file.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: GPU type string "NVIDIA_L4_GPUS" must appear in the implementation — add a source-text assertion in the test.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Minimum threshold must come from yaml_contract.yaml (new field: min_gpu_quota: 1), not hardcoded. If field absent → raise KeyError, not default to 1.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Mocked tests must exercise quota-insufficient path (quota=0 or quota < job_count) returning (False, helpful_message).
task.rule31-authorization: Adding min_gpu_quota field to yaml_contract.yaml is authorized by issue #943. This adds a new field — it does not change existing values.

tdd-approach.red: Add test class TestGpuQuotaPreflight to test_preflight_quota.py: - test_gpu_quota_function_exists (ast.parse, checks function name) - test_gpu_quota_wired_in_checks_list (linear scan of checks = [...]) - test_gpu_quota_checks_l4_string (assert "NVIDIA_L4_GPUS" in source) - test_gpu_quota_passes_when_quota_sufficient (mock _run returns json with L4 quota limit=8, usage=2 → (True, msg)) - test_gpu_quota_warns_when_quota_below_job_count (quota=1, 34 jobs → (True, "WARNING" in msg)) - test_gpu_quota_fails_when_quota_zero (quota=0 → (False, msg)) - test_gpu_quota_graceful_when_gcloud_missing (_run returncode=127 → (True, "skipped" in msg)) Run: expect FAIL (function missing)
tdd-approach.green: 1. Add min_gpu_quota: 1 to yaml_contract.yaml (authorized by #943) 2. Implement check_gcp_gpu_quota() in preflight_gcp.py: - Reads yaml_contract.yaml for min_gpu_quota (no hardcoded 1) - Runs: gcloud compute regions describe europe-west4 --format=json - Parses json.loads() response for "NVIDIA_L4_GPUS" quota - Returns (True, warning_msg) if quota < job_count (warn, not block) - Returns (False, fail_msg) if quota == 0 - Returns (True, skip_msg) if gcloud unavailable 3. Wire into checks = [...] after check_docker_image_freshness

verification.command: uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py 2>&1 | grep -E "GPU quota"

task.title: Region injection YAML validity test
task.description:
Extend tests/v2/unit/cloud/test_region_injection.py with contract enforcement tests. Read existing tests first to avoid duplication.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: test_generated_yaml_preserves_job_recovery must check content["resources"]["job_recovery"], not top-level "job_recovery".
REVIEWER CORRECTION: test_no_t4_in_ordered must check T4 is not a substring of any accelerator name (e.g., "nvidia-tesla-t4-ultra").
REVIEWER CORRECTION: test_ordered_block_gpus_in_contract_allowlist must also verify that all allowlisted GPUs appear in ordered: block (completeness, not just correctness).
REVIEWER NOTE: test_preserves_non_resource_keys already exists — verify before writing test_generated_yaml_preserves_file_mounts.

tdd-approach.red: Add TestRegionInjectionContractEnforcement class with: - test_generated_yaml_preserves_job_recovery — checks result["resources"]["job_recovery"] key present - test_no_t4_or_banned_in_ordered — no accelerator name contains "T4", "V100", "K80", "P100", "P4", "A100" as substrings - test_ordered_gpu_completeness — every GPU in contract allowlist appears in ordered: block (reads yaml_contract.yaml) - test_required_keys_in_generated_yaml — verify "name", "resources", "file_mounts", "setup", "run" all present Run: expect some FAIL (new assertions against current behavior)
tdd-approach.green: Fix any region_injection.py gaps identified by RED failures. If no gaps → tests go GREEN immediately (regression guards).

task.title: MLflow URI placeholder detection in preflight (touches preflight_gcp.py — runs AFTER 1.2)
task.description:
Extend check_env_vars() in preflight_gcp.py to detect: 1. Unexpanded ${...} placeholders — hard FAIL 2. REPLACE_WITH_... placeholders — hard FAIL 3. Empty string values for required vars — hard FAIL 4. localhost URIs for MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI — soft WARN (True + warning)
REVIEWER CORRECTION: localhost is a warning (True, warning_msg), not fail. Cloud Run dev environments may legitimately tunnel via localhost.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: test_replace_with_placeholder_rejected must cover MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_URI, not just HF_TOKEN.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Add test for partial placeholder: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_HOST:5000

tdd-approach.red: Create test_preflight_env_vars.py with TestEnvVarValidation class: - test_unexpanded_dollar_placeholder_fails (HF_TOKEN=${HF_TOKEN} → False) - test_replace_with_placeholder_fails_for_mlflow (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_URI → False) - test_replace_with_placeholder_fails_for_hf_token (HF_TOKEN=REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_TOKEN → False) - test_partial_placeholder_fails (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_HOST:5000 → False) - test_localhost_uri_returns_warning_not_fail (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://localhost:5000 → True with "localhost" in msg) - test_valid_cloud_run_uri_passes (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=https://mlflow-xxx.run.app → True) - test_empty_required_var_fails (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI= → False) Run: expect FAIL (logic not yet extended)
tdd-approach.green: Extend check_env_vars() in preflight_gcp.py: Use str.startswith("${") and "REPLACE_WITH_" in value.upper() for placeholder detection — no regex (Rule #16). localhost: use str host comparison after urlsplit() or str.split("//")[1].split(":")[0].

verification.command: uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py 2>&1 | grep -E "env vars"

phase.name: P1 High-Value Fixes
phase.description: P1 tasks grouped by shared file dependencies. Groups with no shared files can run in parallel. Groups sharing files must be sequential. Execution order: Step 4: Groups 2.A, 2.D, 2.E, 2.F in parallel Step 5: Groups 2.B, 2.C in parallel (after Step 4 commits)

task.title: SkyPilot YAML hardening — all train_factorial.yaml changes batched
task.description:
Batch all train_factorial.yaml modifications into ONE task to prevent merge conflicts. Changes authorized by issues #946, #948, #953, #955, #956:
1. (#946) Remove || true from HF downloads, add retry loops 2. (#948) Add retry logic for DVC pull and MLflow connectivity check 3. (#953) Wrap dvc pull, HF download, MLflow check with timeout 4. (#955) Add job_recovery.strategy: EAGER_NEXT_REGION explicitly 5. (#956) Add recover_on_exit_codes: [33, 34] to job_recovery
REVIEWER CORRECTION (critical): Before modifying YAML for #955/#956, verify current SkyPilot version supports these fields: uv run python -c "import sky; print(sky.__version__)" Check SkyPilot changelog for job_recovery.strategy and recover_on_exit_codes. If NOT supported → skip those two changes and report to user.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: str.splitlines() then per-line str.split() for setup block parsing — NOT str.split() directly on the whole string.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: "timeout" check must verify the COMMAND token is "timeout", not just that the word appears in the line. Use: line_parts = line.strip().split() and line_parts[0] == "timeout"
REVIEWER CORRECTION: No xfail. If a change is blocked by Rule #31 and the issue does not authorize it, use pytest.skip() not xfail.
EXIT CODE CONVENTION (for #956): 33 = CUDA_INIT_FAILURE (transient, recover) 34 = NETWORK_TIMEOUT (transient, recover) 35 = CHECKPOINT_CORRUPT (not transient, do NOT recover) 137 = OOM/SIGKILL (NOT in recover_on_exit_codes)
task.rule31-authorizations: #946: Remove || true from HF commands — authorized #948: Add DVC/HF retry loops — authorized #953: Add timeout guards — authorized #955: Add job_recovery.strategy — authorized (verify SkyPilot version first) #956: Add recover_on_exit_codes — authorized (verify SkyPilot version first)

tdd-approach.green:
STEP 0 (before any YAML change): Check SkyPilot version: uv run python -c "import sky; print(sky.__version__)" If version < 0.7.0 (where strategy was added): skip #955/#956 changes, add pytest.skip() to those tests, report to user.
STEP 1: Apply changes to train_factorial.yaml: - Remove `|| true` from all HF commands - Add retry loops (3 attempts, backoff) for HF downloads - Add retry loops for DVC pull - Add timeout NNN before each long-running command - If SkyPilot supports it: add job_recovery.strategy and recover_on_exit_codes
STEP 2: Verify YAML is still valid: yaml.safe_load() on the file.

verification.command: uv run python -c "import yaml,pathlib; yaml.safe_load(pathlib.Path('deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml').read_text())" && echo "YAML valid"

task.title: run_factorial.sh robustness — lockfile, resume fix, permanent failure tracking
task.description:
Three changes to run_factorial.sh batched together to prevent conflicts.
1. (#950) Fix resume substring collision: change grep -qF to grep -xF so "dynunet-dice_ce" does not match "dynunet-dice_ce_cldice"
2. (#951) Add lockfile mechanism: outputs/.factorial.lock with PID. Check if lock PID is still running on startup. Remove on EXIT trap.
3. (#947) Permanent failure tracking in run_factorial_resilient.sh: parse job log for per-condition FAILED count. Skip after 3 FAILs.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Behavioral tests required for #950, not just source inspection. Tests must construct mock sky jobs queue output containing names where one is a substring of another, and verify the grep behavior correctly identifies each independently.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Lockfile test must verify lock appears BEFORE the first sky jobs launch call by comparing line indices.

tdd-approach.red:
Read current state of test_run_factorial_resilience.py FIRST. Add to existing file (do not overwrite):
class TestResumeCollisionFix: def test_resume_uses_exact_line_match(self): """Source uses grep -xF, not grep -qF.""" src = Path("scripts/run_factorial.sh").read_text(encoding="utf-8") lines = src.splitlines() # Find the resume grep line and check it uses -xF resume_lines = [l for l in lines if "grep" in l and "CONDITION_NAME" in l] assert len(resume_lines) > 0, "No resume grep line found" assert all("-xF" in l or "--line-regexp" in l for l in resume_lines), ( "Resume grep must use -xF (exact line match), not -qF" ) def test_substring_collision_prevented(self, tmp_path): """Mock sky jobs queue output with substring collision.""" # Construct fake queue output where "dynunet-dice_ce" appears # as a substring of "dynunet-dice_ce_cldice" queue_output = "
".join([ "5 - dynunet-dice_ce_cldice-calibtrue-f0 ... SUCCEEDED", "4 - dynunet-dice_ce-calibtrue-f0 ... SUCCEEDED", ]) # grep -xF "dynunet-dice_ce" should NOT match the cldice line import subprocess result = subprocess.run( ["grep", "-xF", "dynunet-dice_ce-calibtrue-f0"], input="
".join(l.split()[2] for l in queue_output.splitlines()), capture_output=True, text=True, ) # Should match exactly 1 line assert result.stdout.count("
") == 1
class TestLockfileGuard: def test_lockfile_created(self): src = Path("scripts/run_factorial.sh").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "factorial.lock" in src or ".lock" in src def test_lockfile_before_launch(self): """Lock must appear before sky jobs launch in script.""" lines = Path("scripts/run_factorial.sh").read_text().splitlines() lock_idx = next((i for i, l in enumerate(lines) if ".lock" in l), -1) launch_idx = next((i for i, l in enumerate(lines) if "sky jobs launch" in l), -1) assert lock_idx < launch_idx, "Lockfile must precede sky jobs launch" def test_lock_uses_pid(self): src = Path("scripts/run_factorial.sh").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "$$" in src or "$BASHPID" in src # PID in lockfile
class TestPermanentFailureDetection: def test_permanent_failure_threshold_from_config(self): """Max failure count must come from factorial YAML, not hardcoded.""" src = Path("scripts/run_factorial_resilient.sh").read_text() # Must not contain hardcoded integer like MAX_FAILS=3 # (unless it reads from config YAML) assert "PERMANENTLY_FAILED" in src or "permanent_fail" in src.lower() def test_permanent_failure_skips_condition(self, tmp_path): """Condition with 3 FAILs in log must be skipped on resume.""" log = tmp_path / "job_ids.txt" log.write_text( "1 | sam3_topolora-dice_ce-calibtrue-f0 | LAUNCH_FAILED
" "2 | sam3_topolora-dice_ce-calibtrue-f0 | LAUNCH_FAILED
" "3 | sam3_topolora-dice_ce-calibtrue-f0 | LAUNCH_FAILED
", encoding="utf-8" ) # Import the detection function (must exist after implementation) import importlib.util spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location( "resilient", "scripts/run_factorial_resilient.sh" ) # For bash scripts, check via pattern analysis src = Path("scripts/run_factorial_resilient.sh").read_text() assert "PERMANENTLY_FAILED" in src
Run: expect FAIL (features not yet implemented)
tdd-approach.green: 1. run_factorial.sh: Change grep -qF to grep -xF in resume section 2. run_factorial.sh: Add lockfile at outputs/.factorial.lock - Create: echo $$ > outputs/.factorial.lock - Check: if [ -f lock ] && kill -0 $(cat lock) 2>/dev/null; then exit 1; fi - Remove: trap "rm -f outputs/.factorial.lock" EXIT 3. run_factorial_resilient.sh: Track per-condition FAILED count - Parse all job log files for condition name + LAUNCH_FAILED entries - After 3 failures for same condition: mark PERMANENTLY_FAILED in log - On --resume: skip PERMANENTLY_FAILED conditions

task.title: Controller health preflight check (#957)
task.description:
Add check_controller_health() to preflight_gcp.py. Detects INIT/STOPPED controller state before launch.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Mock for sky unavailability must use subprocess returncode=127 (command not found), not just returncode=1.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: test for INIT state returns (True, warning) — not (False, fail). INIT may resolve within seconds. STOPPED/ERROR → fail.

tdd-approach.red: Create test file with TestControllerHealthCheck class: - test_controller_health_function_exists (ast.parse) - test_controller_health_wired_in_checks (linear scan) - test_controller_up_returns_pass (_run mock: "sky-controller UP") - test_controller_init_returns_warning (_run mock: "sky-controller INIT" → True, "INIT" in msg) - test_controller_stopped_returns_fail (_run mock: "STOPPED" → False) - test_controller_not_created_returns_pass (_run: empty output → True) - test_controller_graceful_when_sky_missing (_run returncode=127 → True) Run: expect FAIL
tdd-approach.green: Implement check_controller_health() following existing check pattern. Parse sky status output via str.split() on lines — no regex. Wire into checks = [...] list.

task.title: Atomic checkpoint save + corruption fallback (#949)
task.description:
Add atomic_torch_save() (write-to-tmp, fsync, rename) and load_checkpoint_with_fallback() (try latest, fall back to previous epoch).
All five critical scenarios must be tested: - Happy path (valid primary) - Corruption fallback (truncated bytes) - Double corruption (both fail → raise) - Warning logging on fallback - Missing fallback (FileNotFoundError)
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Add test for non-pickle file (JSON file saved as .pth) → verify RuntimeError caught and fallback attempted.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: test_logs_warning_on_fallback must assert str(fallback_path) in caplog.text, not just "fallback" in caplog.text.

tdd-approach.red: Create test_atomic_checkpoint.py with: - test_atomic_save_produces_identical_file (tmp_path) - test_atomic_save_no_partial_file_on_crash (mock rename to raise, verify no partial .pth file remains) - test_fallback_loads_previous_when_latest_truncated - test_fallback_loads_previous_when_latest_not_pickle (write JSON as .pth) - test_double_corruption_raises_value_error - test_logs_warning_with_fallback_path (caplog, assert str(fallback) in msg) - test_missing_fallback_raises_file_not_found - test_all_checkpoint_saves_use_atomic (ast.parse of training_loop.py, verify no direct torch.save() calls) Run: expect FAIL
tdd-approach.green: Add to checkpoint_utils.py: - atomic_torch_save(obj, path: Path) — write to path.with_suffix(".tmp"), fsync, os.rename() (atomic on POSIX) - load_checkpoint_with_fallback(primary: Path, fallback: Path | None) Update training_loop.py to use atomic_torch_save().
tdd-approach.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/pipeline/test_atomic_checkpoint.py -v uv run mypy src/minivess/pipeline/checkpoint_utils.py

task.title: MLflow artifact GCS store test (#952) — RULE #31 STOP POINT
task.description:
RULE #31 STOP POINT: yaml_contract.yaml does NOT currently have a mlflow_artifact_bucket field. Adding it is a declarative config change requiring explicit user authorization.
STOP AND ASK THE USER: "Issue #952 requires adding mlflow_artifact_bucket to yaml_contract.yaml. This is a Rule #31 config change. Is this authorized?"
If NOT authorized: implement only the tests that do NOT require the new contract field: 1. test_train_factorial_env_has_mlflow_artifact_root — verifies train_factorial.yaml envs section has MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_BUCKET or MLFLOW_DEFAULT_ARTIFACT_ROOT pointing to gs:// 2. test_train_flow_uses_artifact_store — ast.parse of train_flow.py, verify mlflow.log_artifact() is called (not mlflow.log_dict() for large files)
If authorized: also add: 3. test_yaml_contract_has_mlflow_bucket_field 4. test_gcs_uri_no_trailing_slash (from yaml_contract.yaml field)
REVIEWER CORRECTION: The Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp uses ENV VAR MLFLOW_DEFAULT_ARTIFACT_ROOT set by Pulumi, not hardcoded in the Dockerfile. Do NOT test the Dockerfile for hardcoded bucket name. Test the Pulumi output or the train_factorial.yaml envs section instead.

tdd-approach.red: Create test file with the ALWAYS-SAFE tests (no config change needed): - test_train_factorial_has_artifact_bucket_env — yaml.safe_load() train_factorial.yaml, check envs section contains key that starts with "MLFLOW" and value starts with "gs://" - test_train_flow_uses_log_artifact — ast.parse train_flow.py, verify at least one call to mlflow.log_artifact() exists - test_artifact_bucket_is_gcs_uri — if bucket env var found, assert value starts with "gs://" and does not end with "/" Run: expect FAIL if envs section doesn't have GCS bucket var
tdd-approach.green: Implement tests. If yaml_contract.yaml change is authorized, also add mlflow_artifact_bucket field and corresponding tests.

task.title: .env SkyPilot format compatibility test (#954)
task.description:
REVIEWER CORRECTION: str.isidentifier() is WRONG for POSIX env var names. Use POSIX check instead: - Valid key: starts with letter or underscore, contains only A-Z, 0-9, _ - Check: import string; all(c in string.ascii_letters + string.digits + "_" for c in key) and not key[0].isdigit() - Do NOT use key.isupper() — lowercase is technically valid in env files.
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Handle multi-line values (KEY=value\\ continuation). Detect and skip continuation lines (lines starting with spaces or ending with \\).
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Test that no key appears twice (duplicates silently overwrite, causing non-deterministic behavior).
REVIEWER CORRECTION: Empty value for required var = hard FAIL, not advisory warning. Required vars are defined by REQUIRED_ENV_VARS in preflight_gcp.py.

tdd-approach.red: Create test file with: - test_all_keys_valid_posix (import string, POSIX check) - test_no_quoted_values_with_double_quotes (no KEY="value") - test_no_quoted_values_with_single_quotes (no KEY='value') - test_no_export_prefix (no "export KEY=value") - test_no_inline_comments_after_value (no KEY=value # comment) - test_no_duplicate_keys (keys counted, len == len(set)) - test_required_vars_have_non_empty_values (REQUIRED_ENV_VARS from preflight_gcp.py, value != "") - test_continuation_lines_handled (lines ending with \\ are skipped) Run: expect some FAIL if .env.example has format issues
tdd-approach.green: Fix any .env.example format violations found by RED phase.

task.title: Condition name uniqueness test (#958)
task.description: REVIEWER CORRECTION: Add 63-char Kubernetes label length limit check. REVIEWER CORRECTION: Test names across model families for prefix collisions (sam3 vs sam3_topolora vs sam3_hybrid). REVIEWER CORRECTION: Verify Python name generator matches bash name format (same separators, same calib prefix).

tdd-approach.red: Tests: - test_all_condition_names_unique (Cartesian product, set check) - test_no_name_is_substring_of_another (all pairs) - test_no_cross_model_prefix_collision (sam3 vs sam3_topolora) - test_names_within_k8s_label_limit (all len <= 63) - test_names_have_no_spaces, test_names_have_no_slashes - test_python_format_matches_bash_format (generate with python using same logic as bash, compare)
tdd-approach.green: Pure guard — configs should already pass. Fix if RED fails.

task.title: Parallel backpressure test (#959)
task.description: Read test_run_factorial_resilience.py AFTER 2.B's changes before adding tests. REVIEWER CORRECTION: Test must verify PARALLEL_SUBMISSIONS read from YAML, not hardcoded. Check no integer literal assigned to PARALLEL_SUBMISSIONS.

tdd-approach.red: Add TestParallelBackpressure class (after reading current file state): - test_parallel_submissions_read_from_yaml (no integer literal in bash) - test_parallel_submissions_matches_cloud_config (value in script matches cloud config YAML value) Behavioral test via mock sky binary is deferred (out of scope for staging tier).

task.title: WeightWatcher 3D graceful handling (#960)
task.description: run_weightwatcher() must not crash on Conv3d — return {} or partial metrics.

tdd-approach.red: Add to test_weightwatcher_diagnostics.py: - test_weightwatcher_3d_only_model_returns_empty (Conv3d only → {}) - test_weightwatcher_mixed_3d_linear_returns_partial (Conv3d+Linear → metrics) - test_weightwatcher_3d_no_exception_raised
tdd-approach.green: Wrap Conv3d layer analysis in try/except in weight_diagnostics.py. Log warning and skip Conv3d layers. Return partial metrics.

task.title: Preflight checks mocked unit tests (#961)
task.description: Add behavioral mocked tests for existing preflight checks that currently only have structural tests. Follow test_docker_freshness_gate.py pattern.

tdd-approach.red: Parametrized tests covering: check_gcs_bucket, check_checkpoint_bucket, check_dvc_data_on_gcs, check_skypilot_gcp, check_yaml_contract, check_gcp_cpu_quota. Each test: mock _run success + failure, verify (bool, str) tuple with content-specific assertions. Add: malformed JSON from gcloud → returns (True, "skipped") not exception.
tdd-approach.green: Fix any preflight checks that raise on malformed input.

task.title: Preemption rate monitoring script (#962)
task.description: Create scripts/analyze_factorial_run.py that parses job log files and sky jobs queue JSON to compute preemption metrics per pass. Appends to outputs/harness-state.jsonl.

tdd-approach.red: Test with synthetic job log data: - test_preemption_count_correct (3 recoveries across jobs → count=3) - test_preemption_rate_correct (3/10 jobs = 30%) - test_cost_overhead_computed (wall_time - job_time = overhead) - test_empty_log_raises_value_error (Rule #25) - test_output_appended_to_harness_state (json.loads on each line)
tdd-approach.green: Implement using json.loads() on sky jobs queue --format json output. Rule #16: no regex. Rule #25: raise on empty input. Rule #7: datetime.now(timezone.utc) for timestamps.

task.title: Training heartbeat zombie detection (#963)
task.description:
REVIEWER CORRECTION (critical): Do NOT run actual training at staging tier. Phase 1 (structural, staging-safe): Use ast.parse() to verify heartbeat log call exists in train_flow.py source. Phase 2 (behavioral, model_loading tier): Run 2 mock epochs, verify heartbeat file written. Mark @pytest.mark.model_loading.
Heartbeat: train_flow.py writes {LOGS_DIR}/heartbeat.json with {"epoch": N, "timestamp": "..."} after each epoch.

tdd-approach.red:
Create test_training_heartbeat.py with TWO test classes:
class TestHeartbeatStructural: # staging-safe, no model loading def test_heartbeat_write_in_train_flow_source(self): """ast.parse() verifies heartbeat.json write in train_flow.py.""" src = Path("src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py") tree = ast.parse(src.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) # Find any string constant "heartbeat" in the AST constants = [n.value for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.Constant) and isinstance(n.value, str)] assert any("heartbeat" in c for c in constants), ( "train_flow.py must write heartbeat.json per epoch" ) def test_heartbeat_stale_detection_logic(self): """Stale heartbeat detection returns True for >30min old.""" from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone old_ts = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=31)).isoformat() # The detection function (to be created in train_flow.py) from minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow import is_heartbeat_stale assert is_heartbeat_stale({"timestamp": old_ts}) is True def test_fresh_heartbeat_not_stale(self): from datetime import datetime, timezone from minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow import is_heartbeat_stale fresh_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat() assert is_heartbeat_stale({"timestamp": fresh_ts}) is False
@pytest.mark.model_loading class TestHeartbeatBehavioral: def test_heartbeat_file_written_after_epoch(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch): """Behavioral: verify heartbeat.json written after each epoch.""" # This test is excluded from make test-staging via model_loading marker pass # Implementation deferred — requires mock training loop
Run structural tests: expect FAIL (is_heartbeat_stale not yet in train_flow.py)
tdd-approach.green: Add to train_flow.py: - is_heartbeat_stale(heartbeat: dict) → bool (30 min threshold from config) - Write {"epoch": N, "timestamp": utc_iso} to LOGS_DIR/heartbeat.json after each epoch Rule #7: datetime.now(timezone.utc) — never datetime.now()

task.title: Spot/on-demand fallback design document (#964)
task.description:
REVIEWER CORRECTION: This is an explicitly requested document (issue #964 explicitly requests it), meeting the Rule #15 exception for documentation. Per the issue acceptance criteria, this is a RESEARCH + DECISION task, not an implementation task.
IMPORTANT: Use test_deployment_docs.py (NOT test_design_docs.py — wrong path). Read the existing file first, then add a test class.
NO YAML CHANGES until user decision. Document research only.

tdd-approach.green: Create docs/planning/spot-ondemand-fallback-design.md with: - Cost comparison: spot vs on-demand for debug ($2.50 vs $5.50) and production ($87 vs $220) - SkyPilot any_of configuration syntax - yaml_contract.yaml changes required (allow_ondemand_fallback field) - Recommendation section (user fills in decision) - References to SkyPilot auto-failover docs Note: No YAML changes until user explicitly authorizes (Rule #31).

final-gate.command: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ 2>&1 | tail -5
